title: Verdict Conflict Resolution (v0.1)
description: v0.217: a substrate primitive for resolving contradicting verdicts on overlapping Diff Pack members. When reviewer A issues verdict=accept on pack P and reviewer B issues verdict=reject on pack P', and P and P' share a member proposal, the substrate records the conflict and an explicit resolution (majority, owner-override, escalation). Pinned by Lean Theorem 31 (verdict-conflict id injectivity).

verdicts.description: The contradicting pending-verdict ids (vpv_*) involved in the conflict.

shared_member_ids.description: The vpr_* proposal ids that appear in more than one of the conflicting packs.

resolution_mode.description: How the conflict was resolved. majority: most verdicts of one outcome win; owner_override: the frontier owner's verdict supersedes peers; escalation: handed up to a higher-authority reviewer.

resolution_actor.description: The actor whose decision the resolution records. For majority mode, this is the actor authorizing the count.

winning_verdict_id.description: Optional: the pending-verdict that won under the resolution. May be null when the resolution opens a new review cycle rather than picking a side.

rationale.description: Free-text rationale recorded by the resolver.
